title: All stream names reference canonical RedisStreams constants
evidence: Grep for hardcoded 'stream:...' in services/*/src/**/*.ts and shared/core/src/**/*.ts returned 0 matches. All XADD/XREADGROUP call sites use RedisStreams.* or RedisStreamsClient.STREAMS.* constants.
expected: No hardcoded stream name strings in non-test source files
actual: No hardcoded stream name strings found
recommendation: No action needed. Continue enforcing via code review.

title: All consumer groups follow naming convention and match expected list
evidence: Found 7 consumer groups: coordinator-group (coordinator.ts:396), execution-engine-group (opportunity.consumer.ts:175, fast-lane.consumer.ts:103), cross-chain-detector-group (detector.ts:333,346,353), mempool-detector-group (mempool-config.ts:78), orderflow-pipeline (orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts:81), self-healing-manager (expert-self-healing-manager.ts:38), failover-{serviceName} (cross-region-health.ts:750). All match expected list.
expected: Only expected consumer group names in codebase
actual: All 7 consumer groups match expected list and <service>-group convention
recommendation: No action needed.

title: All 24 declared streams have MAXLEN values in STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS
evidence: STREAM_MAX_LENGTHS in streams.ts:404-432 defines limits for all 24 RedisStreams constants. Coverage verified against events.ts:19-61.
expected: All 24 streams have MAXLEN entries
actual: All 24 streams covered (PRICE_UPDATES:100000, OPPORTUNITIES:500000, HEALTH:1000, EXECUTION_REQUESTS:25000, etc.)
recommendation: No action needed. Fallback DEFAULT_STREAM_MAX_LENGTH=5000 handles unknown streams with a once-per-stream warning.

title: All XADD calls use xaddWithLimit — no raw xadd bypasses
evidence: All service source files use streamsClient.xaddWithLimit(). No direct redis.xadd() or client.xadd() calls found in services/*/src/**/*.ts. In shared/core/src, the only this.xadd() call is inside xaddWithLimit() itself (streams.ts:772). StreamBatcher.flush() uses this.client.xaddWithLimit() (streams.ts:290). DLQ writes use xaddWithLimit() (streams.ts:942).
expected: No raw xadd calls bypassing MAXLEN enforcement
actual: All publish paths go through xaddWithLimit()
recommendation: No action needed.

title: All consumer group readers properly ACK messages
evidence: xreadgroup usage traced in 4 shared/core files: streams.ts (ACK at line 927,972), stream-consumer.ts (ACK at line 296,441), orderflow-pipeline-consumer.ts (ACK at line 342), cross-region-health.ts (ACK at line 808). Service-level consumers use StreamConsumer which handles ACK internally. DLQ consumer uses xread (not xreadgroup) so no ACK needed.
expected: Every xreadgroup call has corresponding xack
actual: All consumer group readers have proper ACK
recommendation: No action needed.

title: All service ports match registry with no collisions
evidence: Verified ports: coordinator=3000 (index.ts:72), execution-engine=3005 (index.ts:50), cross-chain=3006 (index.ts:25), unified-detector=3007 (constants.ts:67), mempool=3008 (types.ts:447), monolith=3100 (index.ts:65), coordinator-worker=3009 (index.ts:107). Partitions use PARTITION_PORTS from service-ports.json: asia-fast=3001, l2-turbo=3002, high-value=3003, solana=3004.
expected: All ports match shared/constants/service-ports.json with no duplicates
actual: All 11 service ports match registry. No collisions detected.
recommendation: No action needed.

title: Environment variable drift summary: 183 undocumented, 46 orphaned
evidence: 183 env vars used in code (services/ + shared/) but not documented in .env.example. 46 env vars documented in .env.example but not referenced in code. Breakdown: 20 CRITICAL (secrets/keys), 41 HIGH (thresholds/limits/timeouts), 122 MEDIUM (other config).
expected: All env vars used in code should be documented in .env.example and vice versa
actual: Significant bidirectional drift between code and documentation. 183 undocumented vars include private keys (ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BSC_PRIVATE_KEY, etc.), auth tokens (JWT_SECRET, DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN), and operational config (STREAM_SIGNING_KEY, REDIS_PASSWORD). 46 orphaned vars include GAS_PRICE_*_GWEI (12 chains), NONCE_POOL_*, PROVIDER_* timeouts.
recommendation: Audit and add missing env vars to .env.example. Remove orphaned vars or add code references. Priority: document all CRITICAL (secret) vars first.

title: 20 secret/key env vars undocumented in .env.example
evidence: ARBITRUM_PRIVATE_KEY, BASE_PRIVATE_KEY, BSC_PRIVATE_KEY, ETHEREUM_PRIVATE_KEY, POLYGON_PRIVATE_KEY, WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY, WALLET_MNEMONIC, WALLET_MNEMONIC_PASSPHRASE, JWT_SECRET, KMS_KEY_ID, DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN, HEALTH_AUTH_TOKEN, API_KEYS, REDIS_PASSWORD, TENDERLY_API_KEY, WEBHOOK_SECRET, STREAM_SIGNING_KEY, STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS, RISK_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX, SOLANA_WALLET_PUBLIC_KEY
expected: All secret/key env vars should be documented in .env.example with clear instructions to put them in .env.local only
actual: 20 secret/key env vars referenced in code are missing from .env.example. Operators deploying from .env.example will not know these vars exist.
recommendation: Add all 20 vars to .env.example as commented-out entries with instructions to set in .env.local. Per-chain private keys are especially critical for multi-chain deployment.

title: 41 threshold/limit/timeout env vars undocumented in .env.example
evidence: Includes: MAX_CONCURRENT_EXECUTIONS, MAX_CONCURRENT_PER_CHAIN, WORKER_POOL_SIZE, WORKER_POOL_MAX_QUEUE_SIZE, WORKER_POOL_TASK_TIMEOUT_MS, MEMPOOL_MAX_STREAM_LENGTH, MEMPOOL_MAX_BUFFER_SIZE, MEMPOOL_BATCH_SIZE, MEMPOOL_BATCH_TIMEOUT_MS, FAST_LANE_MIN_CONFIDENCE, FAST_LANE_MIN_PROFIT_USD, CONSECUTIVE_EXPIRED_THRESHOLD, SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS, STATE_TRANSITION_TIMEOUT_MS, MULTI_LEG_TIMEOUT_MS, EVENT_BATCHER_TIMEOUT_MS, etc.
expected: Operational tuning parameters should be documented so operators can adjust for their environment
actual: 41 high-impact tuning parameters are only discoverable by reading source code
recommendation: Document all threshold/limit/timeout vars in .env.example with default values and explanatory comments

title: 46 orphaned env vars in .env.example not referenced in code
evidence: GAS_PRICE_ARBITRUM_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_AVALANCHE_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_BASE_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_BLAST_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_BSC_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_ETHEREUM_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_FANTOM_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_LINEA_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_OPTIMISM_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_POLYGON_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_SCROLL_GWEI, GAS_PRICE_ZKSYNC_GWEI, NONCE_POOL_SIZE, NONCE_POOL_REPLENISH_THRESHOLD, PROVIDER_CONNECTIVITY_TIMEOUT_MS, PROVIDER_HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS, PROVIDER_RECONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS, COMMIT_REVEAL_VALIDATE_PROFIT, CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, COW_BACKRUN_ETH_PRICE_USD, EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_MS, MAX_GAS_PRICE_GWEI, MIN_PROFIT_PERCENTAGE, P1_ASIA_FAST_PORT, P2_L2_TURBO_PORT, P3_HIGH_VALUE_PORT, P4_SOLANA_PORT, etc.
expected: All vars in .env.example should have corresponding code references
actual: 46 vars documented but never read via process.env — may confuse operators or indicate dead code
recommendation: Remove orphaned vars from .env.example or add code references. GAS_PRICE_*_GWEI vars may be consumed via dynamic key construction not caught by static grep.

title: || 0 anti-pattern found in 9 test file locations (0 in production code)
evidence: shared/core/__tests__/unit/cross-chain-simulator.test.ts:268 (bridgeFee || 0), :269 (expectedGasCost || 0), :272 (expectedProfit || 0); shared/core/__tests__/unit/regression-deep-dive.test.ts:723 (timestamp || 0); shared/core/__tests__/unit/tier3-optimizations.test.ts:1048 (confidence || 0); shared/core/__tests__/unit/warming/warming-flow.test.ts:229 (durationUs || 0); shared/config/__tests__/unit/websocket-resilience.test.ts:197,211 (wsFallbackUrls?.length || 0); shared/config/__tests__/unit/token-config-solana-validation.test.ts:615 (decimals[token.decimals] || 0)
expected: Use ?? 0 (nullish coalescing) for numeric defaults, not || 0 which treats 0 as falsy
actual: 9 occurrences of || 0 in test files, 0 in production code. Production code correctly uses ?? 0 pattern. No || 0n found anywhere.
recommendation: Low priority: migrate test files to ?? 0 for consistency. Production code is clean.

title: HMAC signing: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY documented and enforced in production
evidence: shared/core/src/redis/streams.ts:459-464 throws Error in production if signingKey is missing. STREAM_SIGNING_KEY documented at .env.example:620. Empty/whitespace detection at streams.ts:1651-1660. Fail-closed behavior confirmed.
expected: HMAC signing must be enforced in production with fail-closed behavior
actual: Correctly implemented: production throws if STREAM_SIGNING_KEY missing, empty/whitespace values detected and warned, fail-closed enforced.
recommendation: No action needed. Implementation is correct.

title: HMAC key rotation: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS documented and implemented
evidence: STREAM_SIGNING_KEY_PREVIOUS documented at .env.example:622-624 with rotation instructions. Code at streams.ts:1664 reads previous key. Both getRedisStreamsClient and createRedisStreamsClient wire previous key. Legacy compat (LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT) supports migration from pre-OP-18 format.
expected: Key rotation support with previous key acceptance during rotation window
actual: Correctly implemented with documentation and both factory functions wiring the previous key.
recommendation: No action needed. Key rotation is properly supported.

title: LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT env var name mismatch between code and .env.example
evidence: Code reads process.env.LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT at streams.ts:1674 and streams.ts:1735. .env.example documents LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT at line 628. Comment at streams.ts:469 also references LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT.
expected: Env var name in code should match documentation
actual: Code uses LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT but .env.example documents LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT. Setting LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT=false in .env would have NO effect — the code would still use the default value.
recommendation: Rename .env.example line 628 from LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT to LEGACY_HMAC_COMPAT, or update code to read LEGACY_SIGNATURE_COMPAT. This is a silent misconfiguration risk.

title: All 23 feature flags present in code and documented in .env.example
evidence: All 23 expected FEATURE_* flags found in shared/config/src/feature-flags.ts FEATURE_FLAGS object: useBatchedQuoter, useFlashLoanAggregator, useCommitReveal, useCommitRevealRedis, useDestChainFlashLoan, useMomentumTracking, useMLSignalScoring, useSignalCacheRead, useLiquidityDepthSizing, useDynamicL1Fees, useOrderflowPipeline, useKmsSigning, useFastLane, useBackrunStrategy, useUniswapxFiller, useMevShareBackrun, useSolanaExecution, useStatisticalArb, useMevShare, useAdaptiveRiskScoring, useTimeboost, useFlashbotsProtectL2, useCowBackrun. All documented in .env.example.
expected: 23 feature flags in code and .env.example
actual: 23/23 flags present in both locations. Count matches.
recommendation: No action needed.

title: Feature flag cross-dependency validation: SIGNAL_CACHE_READ->ML_SIGNAL_SCORING validated
evidence: feature-flags.ts:653-661 validates useSignalCacheRead requires useMLSignalScoring. feature-flags.ts:664-673 validates useMLSignalScoring benefits from useMomentumTracking. feature-flags.ts:573-598 validates useCommitRevealRedis requires REDIS_URL (throws in production). feature-flags.ts:746-758 validates useSolanaExecution requires SOLANA_RPC_URL (throws in production). feature-flags.ts:775-779 validates useMevShareBackrun requires useMevShare.
expected: Cross-dependencies should be validated at startup
actual: All critical cross-dependencies have validation with appropriate severity (warn in dev, throw in prod for REDIS_URL and SOLANA_RPC_URL).
recommendation: No action needed. Cross-dependency validation is thorough.

title: FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES correctly uses opt-out pattern (enabled by default)
evidence: feature-flags.ts:222: useDynamicL1Fees: process.env.FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES !== 'false'. Comments at lines 213-218 explain rationale: safety-critical flag, disabling causes 2-10x gas underestimation on L2s.
expected: FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES should be ON by default (opt-out pattern)
actual: Correctly implemented with !== 'false' opt-out pattern and thorough documentation of rationale.
recommendation: No action needed. Opt-out pattern is correct for safety-critical feature.

title: All risk configuration env vars documented in .env.example
evidence: All risk vars found in both risk-config.ts and .env.example: RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL, RISK_KELLY_MULTIPLIER (0.5), RISK_MAX_SINGLE_TRADE (0.02), RISK_MIN_EV_THRESHOLD (5000000000000000), RISK_MIN_WIN_PROBABILITY (0.3), RISK_MAX_LOSS_PER_TRADE (100000000000000000), RISK_MAX_DAILY_LOSS, RISK_CAUTION_THRESHOLD, RISK_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOSSES, RISK_RECOVERY_MULTIPLIER, RISK_RECOVERY_WINS_REQUIRED, RISK_HALT_COOLDOWN_MS, RISK_CAUTION_MULTIPLIER, RISK_USE_HISTORICAL_GAS, RISK_DEFAULT_GAS_COST, RISK_DEFAULT_PROFIT_ESTIMATE, RISK_MIN_SAMPLES, RISK_DEFAULT_WIN_PROBABILITY, RISK_MAX_OUTCOMES_PER_KEY, RISK_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS, RISK_OUTCOME_RELEVANCE_MS, RISK_PERSIST_TO_REDIS, RISK_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX, RISK_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED.
expected: All risk vars documented
actual: All risk vars are documented in .env.example with correct default values.
recommendation: No action needed.

title: RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL required in production with validation
evidence: risk-config.ts:271 validates RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL is set in production: if (isProduction && !process.env.RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL) errors.push(...). Throws at line 338 if errors.length > 0. Default 10 ETH for dev only. .env.example:RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL=10000000000000000000 (commented out).
expected: RISK_TOTAL_CAPITAL must be required in production
actual: Correctly required in production via validateRiskConfig(). Throws error with clear message. Default 10 ETH only for development.
recommendation: No action needed. Production enforcement is correct.

title: Cross-validation: defaultWinProbability >= minWinProbability verified
evidence: risk-config.ts:329-335 validates probability.defaultWinProbability >= ev.minWinProbability. Default values: defaultWinProbability=0.5, minWinProbability=0.3. Constraint satisfied (0.5 >= 0.3). .env.example documents relationship at line with comment 'Must be >= RISK_MIN_WIN_PROBABILITY or all new combos are rejected.'
expected: defaultWinProbability >= minWinProbability
actual: Correctly validated at startup. Default values satisfy constraint. Documentation includes cross-reference comment.
recommendation: No action needed. Cross-validation is thorough.

title: 122 medium-priority env vars undocumented in .env.example
evidence: Includes chain RPC/WS URLs (ARBITRUM_RPC_URL, BASE_RPC_URL, BSC_RPC_URL, ETHEREUM_RPC_URL, etc.), Bloxroute config (BLOXROUTE_AUTH_HEADER, BLOXROUTE_WS_ENDPOINT, etc.), OTEL config (OTEL_BATCH_SIZE, OTEL_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS, OTEL_SERVICE_NAME), mempool config (MEMPOOL_CONSUMER_GROUP, MEMPOOL_INCLUDE_ROUTERS, etc.), feature flags (FEATURE_BACKRUN_STRATEGY, FEATURE_DEST_CHAIN_FLASH_LOAN, FEATURE_FAST_LANE, etc. — note: these ARE in .env.example but were flagged due to grep extraction truncating FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L1_FEES to FEATURE_DYNAMIC_L), commit reveal contracts (COMMIT_REVEAL_CONTRACT_*), flash loan aggregator weights (FLASH_LOAN_AGGREGATOR_WEIGHT_*), service identity (SERVICE_NAME, SERVICE_ROLE), simulation config, and various other operational params.
expected: All operational env vars documented
actual: Many chain RPC URLs, Bloxroute params, OTEL config, and operational params lack .env.example entries. Note: some FEATURE_* flags were false positives from grep truncation — actual undocumented count may be ~100.
recommendation: Add chain RPC/WS URL templates, Bloxroute config section, OTEL section, and remaining operational params to .env.example.

title: parseInt/parseFloat in services/shared production code all have NaN protection
evidence: services/execution-engine/src/engine.ts:363-415 uses parseInt/parseFloat with Number.isNaN guards. services/coordinator/src/api/middleware/index.ts:62-65 uses parseInt with Number.isNaN+>0 guards. shared/core/src/caching/hierarchical-cache.ts:55-56 uses parseInt with Number.isInteger guard. shared/core/src/logging/otel-transport.ts:134-140 uses parseInt with Number.isFinite guard. shared/core/src/async/event-batcher.ts:14-15 uses parseInt with Number.isNaN guard. shared/core/src/caching/gas-price-cache.ts:335-336 uses parseFloat with Number.isNaN guard. shared/config/src/thresholds.ts:33 uses parseFloat with Number.isNaN guard.
expected: All parseInt/parseFloat(process.env.*) calls should have NaN protection
actual: All 12 parseInt/parseFloat(process.env.*) calls in services/ and shared/ have NaN guards (Number.isNaN, Number.isFinite, Number.isInteger, or || fallback). Migration to parseEnvIntSafe/parseEnvFloatSafe is also in progress (base.strategy.ts, chain-instance.ts, auth.ts).
recommendation: No action needed. Good pattern compliance.

title: parseInt in contracts/ scripts lacks NaN guard
evidence: contracts/hardhat.config.ts:94 parseInt(process.env.FORK_BLOCK_NUMBER) — no radix parameter, no NaN guard. contracts/scripts/deploy-v3-adapter.ts:101 parseInt(process.env.DEFAULT_FEE_TIER ?? '3000', 10) — has ?? fallback but NaN if env var set to non-numeric.
expected: All parseInt(process.env.*) should have NaN guard
actual: Two parseInt calls in contracts/ lack explicit NaN protection. The hardhat.config one is guarded by the ternary (only called when FORK_BLOCK_NUMBER is truthy and !== 'latest'), but NaN would propagate silently if set to e.g. 'abc'. deploy-v3-adapter has ?? '3000' fallback but user-set non-numeric value produces NaN.
recommendation: Add NaN guard: const raw = parseInt(...); if (Number.isNaN(raw)) throw new Error('Invalid FORK_BLOCK_NUMBER'). Low priority — these are dev/deploy scripts, not hot-path.

title: parseEnvIntSafe/parseEnvFloatSafe adoption progressing
evidence: base.strategy.ts uses parseEnvIntSafe (6 calls) and parseEnvFloatSafe (1 call). chain-instance.ts uses parseEnvIntSafe (4 calls). auth.ts uses parseEnvIntSafe (1 call). engine.ts uses parseEnvIntSafe (3 calls).
expected: Gradual migration to safe utilities
actual: 14 uses of parseEnvIntSafe/parseEnvFloatSafe across 4 production files. Older code in engine.ts still uses inline parseInt+NaN guard pattern. Both patterns are NaN-safe.
recommendation: Continue migration to parseEnvIntSafe/parseEnvFloatSafe for consistency. Low priority — both patterns are functionally equivalent.

title: RedisClient and RedisStreamsClient configuration fully aligned
evidence: client.ts:218 maxRetriesPerRequest:3, lazyConnect:true, retryStrategy: capped exp backoff 200ms..60s (never null), connectTimeout:3000, enableReadyCheck:false, retryDelayOnFailover:100, reconnectOnError: READONLY. streams.ts:501 maxRetriesPerRequest:3, lazyConnect:true, retryStrategy: same capped exp backoff, connectTimeout:3000, enableReadyCheck:false (SA-1K-001 FIX), retryDelayOnFailover:100 (SA-1K-002 FIX), reconnectOnError: READONLY (SA-052 FIX).
expected: All 4 dimensions aligned: retryStrategy, connectTimeout, maxRetriesPerRequest, lazyConnect
actual: All 7 dimensions aligned: retryStrategy (same capped exponential), connectTimeout (3000ms), maxRetriesPerRequest (3), lazyConnect (true), enableReadyCheck (false), retryDelayOnFailover (100), reconnectOnError (READONLY). Previous SA-1K-001/002/052 fixes resolved all divergences.
recommendation: No action needed. Excellent parity.

title: 9 factory parsers silently return null on parse errors without logging
evidence: algebra-parser.ts:53-55, balancer-v2-parser.ts:79-81 and 150-152, curve-parser.ts:92 and 160, solidly-parser.ts:58, trader-joe-parser.ts:55, v2-pair-parser.ts:52, v3-pool-parser.ts:61-63. All have catch(error) { return null; } with no logging.
expected: Parse errors should be logged at debug/warn level for observability
actual: 9 catch blocks in factory-subscription parsers silently return null. These are event log parsers — a malformed log event would be silently dropped with no trace. While individually low impact (one missed pool), systematic silent failures could mask chain-specific issues (e.g., new contract versions with different log signatures).
recommendation: Add this.logger.debug or console.warn in each catch block with the error and log.transactionHash for traceability. These are not hot-path (factory events are infrequent), so logging is safe.

title: path-finding/multi-leg-path-finder.ts silently returns null on simulation error
evidence: multi-leg-path-finder.ts:632-634 catch(error) { return null; } in simulateSwap(). No logging of the simulation failure.
expected: Swap simulation errors should be logged for debugging
actual: When simulateSwap fails (e.g., overflow, invalid pool state), it silently returns null. The caller skips this path. While functionally correct (failed simulation = skip path), persistent simulation failures for specific pools would be invisible.
recommendation: Add debug-level logging: this.logger.debug('Swap simulation failed', { pool, error }). Low priority — callers handle null correctly.

title: Health-check ping methods correctly return false without logging
evidence: client.ts:1206 ping() catch returns false. streams.ts:1393 ping() catch returns false. client.ts:1447 checkRedisConnection() returns false.
expected: Ping/healthcheck methods may silently return false
actual: These are intentional — ping() is called frequently by health checks, and logging every transient Redis connectivity blip would be noisy. The caller (health endpoint) handles false by reporting unhealthy status.
recommendation: No action needed. Correct pattern for health checks.

title: All service-layer catch blocks log before returning
evidence: coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:116 logs error before return false. unified-detector/src/chain-instance.ts:2142 logs warn before return null. execution-engine/src/strategies/cross-chain.strategy.ts:194 logs debug before return undefined. All 7 catch-return patterns in services/ production code log before returning.
expected: Service catch blocks should log errors
actual: All service-layer catch blocks that return null/false/undefined also log the error at appropriate levels (error, warn, or debug). Good pattern.
recommendation: No action needed.

title: Total catch block audit: 887 catch blocks in production code, 0 truly empty
evidence: Grep for catch blocks in services/ and shared/ (excluding test files) found 887 catch blocks. Zero empty catch blocks (no catch {} pattern). 9 silent-return-without-log in shared/core parsers (SA-3M-001), 1 in path-finder (SA-3M-002). All others either log, rethrow, or are health-check pings.
expected: No empty catch blocks
actual: No truly empty catch blocks found. 10 catch blocks return without logging (9 parsers + 1 path finder). This is a significant improvement from earlier analysis where empty catches were flagged.
recommendation: Address the 10 silent returns per SA-3M-001 and SA-3M-002.

title: Stream deserialization timestamp and expiresAt fields remain strings after validation
evidence: validation.ts:332-343 restores profitPercentage, confidence, expectedProfit, estimatedProfit via parseFloat. But timestamp (serialized as string on stream-serialization.ts:77) and expiresAt are NOT restored to numbers. Line 347: data as unknown as ArbitrageOpportunity cast means these string fields are typed as number.
expected: All numeric fields should be restored to their TypeScript types after deserialization
actual: 4 of 6 numeric fields are properly restored (profitPercentage, confidence, expectedProfit, estimatedProfit). timestamp remains a string after cast. expiresAt is checked for expiry (converted to number locally) but not written back. JS minus-operator coerces strings to numbers, so Date.now() - timestamp works correctly in practice, but this is fragile type-safety.
recommendation: Add restoration for timestamp: if (typeof data.timestamp === 'string') { data.timestamp = Number(data.timestamp); }. Also consider expiresAt. Low severity because JS coercion handles it, but explicit conversion would be safer.

title: Serialization uses isFinite guards to prevent NaN/Infinity poison messages
evidence: stream-serialization.ts:72-88 guards profitPercentage, confidence, expectedProfit, estimatedProfit with (value != null && isFinite(value)) checks, defaulting to '0' if NaN/Infinity. SA-1N-003 FIX.
expected: Numeric fields should be validated before serialization
actual: All 4 key numeric fields have isFinite guards. This prevents NaN or Infinity values from propagating through Redis Streams as poison messages.
recommendation: No action needed. Good defensive serialization.

title: getOptionalNumber type guard handles string-encoded numerics correctly
evidence: type-guards.ts:61-68 getOptionalNumber() checks typeof === 'number' first, then typeof === 'string' with Number() conversion and NaN guard. SA-1N-001 FIX applied.
expected: Type guards should handle both native numbers and string-encoded numerics from Redis
actual: getOptionalNumber correctly handles both types. Previously only checked typeof === 'number' which would fail for all Redis-sourced values. Fix applied.
recommendation: No action needed.

title: as unknown as ArbitrageOpportunity unsafe cast bypasses type checking
evidence: validation.ts:347 'data as unknown as ArbitrageOpportunity'. After manual parseFloat restoration of 4 fields, the remaining Record<string, unknown> is cast wholesale. Fields like buyPrice (number), sellPrice (number), blockNumber (number), amount (number) from the ArbitrageOpportunity interface remain as strings but are typed as numbers.
expected: Either restore all numeric fields or use a proper deserialization function
actual: Only 4 of ~8 numeric fields are explicitly restored. buyPrice, sellPrice, blockNumber, and other numeric fields remain as strings. The cast masks this type mismatch from TypeScript. Downstream code using these fields may behave unexpectedly (e.g., buyPrice + sellPrice would concatenate strings instead of adding numbers).
recommendation: Create a deserializeOpportunity() function that restores all numeric fields, or at minimum add parseFloat for buyPrice, sellPrice, blockNumber. MEDIUM because unreferenced fields may not cause runtime bugs today, but adding new downstream code that uses them would silently fail.

title: Redis key prefix inventory — 18 documented prefixes with clear ownership
evidence: Key prefixes found in production code: (1) coordinator:leader:lock — coordinator leadership. (2) lock:execution: — execution engine distributed locks. (3) health:<serviceName> — per-service health (client.ts, self-healing-manager.ts). (4) health:system:snapshot — system-wide health (SA-1O-002 FIX). (5) metrics:<service>:<bucket> — performance metrics. (6) price:<chainId>:<pairAddr> — price cache (unified-detector). (7) pair:<chain>:<dex>:<t0>:<t1> — pair cache (pair-cache.ts). (8) bridge:recovery:<bridgeId> — cross-chain bridge recovery. (9) bridge:recovery:corrupt:<key> — corrupt bridge data quarantine. (10) commit-reveal:<chain>:<hash> — commit-reveal state. (11) ratelimit:<id> — rate limiting (default prefix). (12) api:<id> — API rate limiting. (13) arbitrage:<id> — arbitrage rate limiting. (14) auth:<type>:<id> — auth lockout/attempts/blacklist. (15) critical:<id> — critical ops rate limiting. (16) dlq:<id> — dead letter queue entries. (17) risk:probabilities: — risk probability tracker. (18) ab-test:<id> — A/B testing metrics. (19) opp:dedup:<id> — opportunity deduplication. (20) region:health: — cross-region health. (21) cache:l2: — hierarchical L2 cache. Stream names: 20 stream: prefixed names in RedisStreams constant.
expected: Clear prefix registry with no collisions
actual: 21 key prefixes + 20 stream names. All use distinct namespaces with no collisions detected.
recommendation: Consider documenting the key prefix registry in a dedicated file for operational reference.

title: health: prefix used by both per-service health and system snapshot without sub-namespace
evidence: client.ts:1035 uses health:<serviceName> (e.g., health:coordinator). enhanced-health-monitor.ts:544 uses health:system:snapshot (SA-1O-002 FIX). self-healing-manager.ts:734 uses health:<serviceName>.
expected: health: prefix should have consistent sub-namespace hierarchy
actual: health: prefix has two usages: (1) health:<serviceName> for per-service health (e.g., health:coordinator, health:execution-engine), and (2) health:system:snapshot for system-wide snapshot. SA-1O-002 already fixed the previous collision by adding 'system:' sub-namespace. Current state is safe because 'system' is not a valid service name. However, if a service named 'system' were added, it would collide.
recommendation: No immediate action needed. The SA-1O-002 fix resolved the actual collision. Document the 'system' reserved name to prevent future collision.

title: Rate limiter prefixes (ratelimit, api, arbitrage, auth, critical) are not sub-namespaced under a common parent
evidence: rate-limiter.ts creates 4 factory instances with different keyPrefix values: 'api', 'arbitrage', 'auth', 'critical'. Default prefix is 'ratelimit'. These are top-level Redis key namespaces without a common parent prefix.
expected: Rate limiting keys should be namespaced under a common prefix like 'rl:api:', 'rl:auth:'
actual: 5 different top-level prefixes for rate limiting. 'api:' and 'auth:' are short, generic prefixes that could theoretically collide with other subsystems. Currently no collision because auth.ts uses 'auth:blacklist:', 'auth:lockout:', 'auth:attempts:' while rate-limiter uses 'auth:<identifier>' (hashed identifier). The keys don't collide in practice because auth.ts sub-keys have 3 segments (auth:blacklist:token) while rate-limiter uses 2 (auth:hashedId).
recommendation: Consider renaming rate-limiter prefixes to 'rl:api:', 'rl:auth:', etc. to avoid future collision risk. Low priority — no actual collision exists today.

title: ADR-022: No spread operators in tight loops on hot-path files
evidence: Grep for spread operators in price-matrix.ts, provider-latency-tracker.ts, runtime-monitor.ts, execution-engine/src/, unified-detector/src/. Spreads found are in getters ({...this.config}, {...this.stats}), constructor initialization, config merging, and cold-path status/metrics methods. Line 313 health-monitoring-manager.ts has push(...trimmedEntries) inside a for-of loop but in a periodic cleanup function (not hot-path). Line 151 circuit-breaker-manager.ts has spread in a for-of loop but getAllStatus() is a status query. No spreads found in hot-path tight loops (price updates, detection, execution).
expected: No spread operators inside for/while/forEach/map/reduce/filter loops on hot-path code
actual: All spread operators on hot-path files are in cold-path or one-shot contexts — compliant with ADR-022
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-022: RingBuffer uses Float64Array with O(1) record()
evidence: Line 71: buffer field is Float64Array (line 80: this.buffer = new Float64Array(capacity)). record() method (line 83-94) uses direct index assignment (this.buffer[this.index] = value) and modular increment ((this.index + 1) % this.capacity) — all O(1) operations with no allocation.
expected: Float64Array for ring buffer storage, O(1) record method
actual: Confirmed: Float64Array at line 71/80, O(1) record at lines 83-94
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-033: Stale price window correctly configured per layer
evidence: Cross-chain detector (detector.ts:1486): maxPriceAgeMs defaults to 30000ms via `this.config.maxPriceAgeMs ?? 30000`. PriceMatrix.getPriceWithFreshnessCheck (price-matrix.ts:717): defaults to 5000ms. ADR-033 explicitly documents the 5000ms L1 cache default as intentional (different layer from the 30s cross-chain detection threshold), with per-chain thresholds configurable via caller argument.
expected: Cross-chain detection: 30000ms. L1 cache: 5000ms (intentionally different per ADR-033 table).
actual: Both values match ADR-033 specification exactly
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-002: No direct HTTP inter-service calls found
evidence: Grep for http://localhost:300[0-6] in services/**/*.ts found only: (1) CORS origin configuration in coordinator middleware (line 86: allowedOrigins default includes localhost:3000/3001) — this is browser CORS, not inter-service communication. (2) Test files (middleware.test.ts). All inter-service communication uses Redis Streams per ADR-002.
expected: No direct HTTP calls between services (Redis Streams only per ADR-002)
actual: No inter-service HTTP calls found; only CORS browser origin config and test files
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-018: Circuit breaker failureThreshold defaults to 5
evidence: Default values confirmed at: shared/core/src/resilience/circuit-breaker.ts:408 (failureThreshold: 5), shared/core/src/resilience/self-healing-manager.ts:36 (SYSTEM_CONSTANTS?.circuitBreaker?.defaultFailureThreshold ?? 5), services/execution-engine/src/types.ts:683 (failureThreshold: 5), services/execution-engine/src/index.ts:99 (safeParseInt(env, 5)), services/coordinator/src/alerts/notifier.ts:47 (failureThreshold: 5), services/execution-engine/src/strategies/uniswapx-filler.strategy.ts:188 (failureThreshold: 5), shared/core/src/mev-protection/backrun-bundle-builder.ts:183 (failureThreshold: 5). Only exception: mev-share-event-listener.ts:233 uses maxReconnectAttempts as threshold (by design — CB opens when reconnects exhausted).
expected: Default failureThreshold = 5 per ADR-018
actual: All defaults confirmed at 5. MEV listener uses dynamic threshold (intentional).
recommendation: No action needed

title: ADR-005: SharedArrayBuffer used for primary price storage
evidence: Line 224: private sharedBuffer: SharedArrayBuffer | null. Line 438-439: if typeof SharedArrayBuffer !== 'undefined', this.sharedBuffer = new SharedArrayBuffer(totalBytes). Line 898: 'Optimized SharedArrayBuffer path with Fix #7 sequence counter protocol'. Thread-safe read/write with seqlock pattern documented in file header (lines 15-16). Fallback to ArrayBuffer only when SharedArrayBuffer unavailable.
expected: SharedArrayBuffer used for L1 price storage per ADR-005
actual: Confirmed: SharedArrayBuffer is the primary storage mechanism with seqlock thread safety
recommendation: No action needed

title: Fly.io execution-engine health check port 8080 does not match internal_port 3005
evidence: Lines 42: internal_port = 3005. Line 37: HEALTH_CHECK_PORT = '3005'. Lines 70,79: checks use port = 8080. The SA-1Q-001 fix comment (line 35-36) states HEALTH_CHECK_PORT was changed from 8080 to 3005, but the [checks] section was not updated. The app will listen on 3005 but Fly.io health checks will probe port 8080, which has no listener — causing perpetual health check failure in production.
expected: Health check port should match internal_port (3005)
actual: Health check port = 8080, internal_port = 3005
recommendation: Update infrastructure/fly/execution-engine.toml checks to use port = 3005 instead of port = 8080

title: All Dockerfiles use node:22-alpine — consistent
evidence: All 8 service Dockerfiles (coordinator, execution-engine, cross-chain-detector, unified-detector, partition-asia-fast, partition-l2-turbo, partition-high-value, partition-solana) use FROM node:22-alpine. Matches engines >=22.0.0 in package.json.
expected: node:22-alpine across all Dockerfiles
actual: Confirmed consistent
recommendation: No action needed

title: Fly.io port and health check paths align with service-ports.json
evidence: Fly.io internal_port values: coordinator=3000 (/api/health), coordinator-standby=3000 (/api/health), partition-asia-fast=3001 (/health, /ready), partition-l2-turbo=3002 (/health, /ready), partition-high-value=3003 (/health, /ready), partition-solana=3004 (/health, /ready), cross-chain-detector=3006 (/health), execution-engine=3005 (/health, /ready). All match service-ports.json. Coordinator uses /api/health (Express prefix); others use /health or /ready.
expected: Ports match service-ports.json, coordinator = /api/health, others = /health
actual: All aligned (except execution-engine check port — see SA-4Q1)
recommendation: No action needed

title: Docker Compose port mappings and health checks consistent
evidence: docker-compose.yml: coordinator=3000 (/api/health), unified-detector=3001 (/health), cross-chain=3006 (/health), execution-engine=3005 (/health). docker-compose.partition.yml: All partitions use internal port 3001 (containerized, host-mapped 3011-3016), /health checks correct. docker-compose.testnet.yml: l2-turbo=3002, high-value=3003, cross-chain=3006, execution-engine=3005, coordinator=3000 — all match. All health check intervals standardized to 15s per ADR-007.
expected: Ports and health paths consistent across compose files
actual: Confirmed consistent
recommendation: No action needed

title: Coordinator shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: shutdownTimeoutMs=15000 (index.ts:194). Redis connectTimeout=3000ms (shared/core/src/redis/client.ts:240). closeServerWithTimeout default=5000ms. 15000 > 5000 (server close) and 15000 > 3000 (Redis connect). Hierarchy: shutdown(15s) > server-close(5s) > redis-connect(3s).
expected: shutdown > server-close > redis-connect
recommendation: No action needed

title: Execution engine shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: Total shutdownTimeoutMs = drainTimeoutMs(30000) + POST_DRAIN_CLEANUP_BUFFER_MS(15000) = 45000ms (index.ts:446). SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 (engine.ts:805). Per-disconnect SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 (types.ts:868). Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. Hierarchy: total-shutdown(45s) > drain(30s) > per-disconnect(8s) > redis-connect(3s). SA-1R-005 validates hierarchy at startup.
expected: total-shutdown > drain > per-disconnect > redis-connect
actual: 45000 > 30000 > 8000 > 3000 — consistent with runtime validation
recommendation: No action needed

title: Cross-chain detector shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: Service-level shutdownTimeoutMs=10000 (default from setupServiceShutdown). Per-disconnect timeout=4000ms (detector.ts:515). Two sequential disconnects: 2*4000=8000ms < 10000ms. Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. 10000 > 8000 (total internal) > 4000 (per-disconnect) > 3000 (redis-connect).
expected: service-shutdown > internal-total > per-disconnect > redis-connect
recommendation: No action needed

title: Partition services shutdown hierarchy is consistent
evidence: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=8000 (line 501). Used for both closeServerWithTimeout (line 585) and detector.stop() timeout (line 598). Sequential: 8000+8000=16000ms max. Redis connectTimeout=3000ms. 8000 > 3000 (shutdown step > redis-connect). No outer force-exit timer — each step calls process.exit() directly on timeout.
expected: Per-step timeout > redis-connect
actual: 8000 > 3000 — consistent. No outer force-exit timer (exits directly per-step).
recommendation: No action needed

title: Monolith service shutdown timeout is hardcoded fallback
evidence: monolith/src/index.ts:66: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = safeParseInt(process.env.MONOLITH_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS, 30000). monolith/src/worker-manager.ts:113: this.shutdownTimeoutMs = config.shutdownTimeoutMs ?? 30_000. Both default to 30000ms — configurable via env. The worker-manager per-worker timeout (line 359) is the same as the overall timeout, meaning if one worker hangs, the others may not get shutdown time.
expected: Per-worker timeout < overall shutdown timeout
actual: Per-worker timeout (30s) equals overall shutdown timeout (30s) — a slow worker could consume the entire budget
recommendation: Consider making the per-worker shutdown timeout a fraction of the overall timeout (e.g., shutdownTimeoutMs / maxWorkers) to ensure all workers get a chance to shut down
